perturbation.original_text: 3. RATE Charterer agrees to pay to Owner a fee of $______ per gallon of product transported. Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a monthly basis and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 30 days of invoice date. The fee stated above, unless otherwise adjusted by the parties pursuant to Section 1 above, shall be adjusted annually (both upward and downward), by a factor equal to the amount of increase or decrease, as the case may be, in the Consumer Price Index for the immediately proceeding month of November, over the Consumer Price Index for November of the preceding year. For purposes hereof, the term "Consumer Price Index" shall mean the "Consumer Price Index for Urban Wage Earners and Clerical Workers (1967=100)" specified for "All Items. United States" compiled by the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the United States Department of Labor (the "Index"). In the event the Consumer Price Index shall be converted to a different standard reference base or otherwise revised, the determination of the percentage change shall be made with the use of such conversion factor, formula or table for converting the Consumer Price Index as may be published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics or, if said Bureau shall not publish the same, then as shall be reasonably determined by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RATE Charterer agrees to pay to Owner a fee of $______ per gallon of product transported. Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a monthly basis and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 30 days of invoice date. The fee stated above, unless otherwise adjusted by the parties pursuant to Section 1 above, shall be adjusted annually (both upward and downward), by a factor equal to an amount determined at Owner's sole discretion. In the event the Consumer Price Index shall be converted to a different standard reference base or otherwise revised, the determination of the percentage change shall be made with the use of such conversion factor, formula or table for converting the Consumer Price Index as may be published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics or, if said Bureau shall not publish the same, then as shall be reasonably determined by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification replaces the clear CPI-based adjustment with an adjustment "determined at Owner's sole discretion". This omission of the CPI-based adjustment mechanism, while keeping the reference to annual adjustments based on Section 1 creates an in-text contradiction. The text still mentions adjustments based on Section 1, but it also gives the sole discretion to the owner to adjust the prices. This creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. RATE Charterer agrees to pay to Owner a fee of $______ per gallon of product transported. Owner will invoice such fees to Charterer on a monthly basis and Charterer will pay such invoiced amounts within 30 days of invoice date. The fee stated above, unless otherwise adjusted by the parties pursuant to Section 1 above, shall be adjusted annually (both upward and downward), by a factor equal to an amount determined at Owner's sole discretion. In the event the Consumer Price Index shall be converted to a different standard reference base or otherwise revised, the determination of the percentage change shall be made with the use of such conversion factor, formula or table for converting the Consumer Price Index as may be published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics or, if said Bureau shall not publish the same, then as shall be reasonably determined by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a CPI-based adjustment to the fee, providing a clear mechanism for price changes. The changed text removes this mechanism and grants the Owner sole discretion in determining the adjustment amount. Since the contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, this creates an in-text contradiction because the original commitment to a CPI-based adjustment is removed and replaced with the owner's discretion, causing a direct conflict in how the rate is adjusted, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. The reference to Section 1 is not enough to resolve this ambiguity. This falls under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. COMPLIANCE WITH LAW; INSURANCE: During the Term of this Agreement, Owner shall comply in all material respects with applicable laws, including, without limitation applicable environmental, health, safety and financial responsibility laws, rules and regulations, applicable to the use of the Vessel for bulk crude oil or finished lubricating products transportation. Owner covenants that it will maintain at all times during the Term of this Agreement insurance coverage for sudden and accidental pollution of $500,000,000.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. COMPLIANCE WITH LAW; INSURANCE: During the Term of this Agreement, Owner shall comply with its own internal policies and procedures related to the use of the Vessel for bulk crude oil or finished lubricating products transportation. Owner covenants that it will maintain at all times during the Term of this Agreement insurance coverage for sudden and accidental pollution as it deems necessary.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement to comply with 'applicable laws' to complying with 'internal policies', and changing 'insurance coverage for sudden and accidental pollution of $500,000,000' to 'insurance coverage for sudden and accidental pollution as it deems necessary' the original clause is omitted and replaced with vague references. This creates an in-text contradiction if other clauses assume compliance with external regulations or a specific insurance amount, like clause 3 of Exhibit A.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. COMPLIANCE WITH LAW; INSURANCE: During the Term of this Agreement, Owner shall comply with its own internal policies and procedures related to the use of the Vessel for bulk crude oil or finished lubricating products transportation. Owner covenants that it will maintain at all times during the Term of this Agreement insurance coverage for sudden and accidental pollution as it deems necessary.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the requirement to comply with 'applicable laws' with compliance to 'internal policies,' and replaces a specific insurance amount ($500,000,000) with a subjective standard ('as it deems necessary'). Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the contradiction is self-evident. The original text ensures compliance with external regulations and a set insurance coverage, while the changed text does not. This creates a direct conflict if other clauses assume compliance with external regulations or a specific insurance amount.
